tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

content.text: {"number":57987,"title":"[BillingBenefits] Docs: Update README and CHANGELOG after TypeSpec migration adding new resource types","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits` **Service directory:** `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/` **Triggered by:** PR #57779 by `@haiyuazhang` ## What Changed The billingbenefits management SDK was migrated from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec (MPG migration). This migration added **8 new resource types** to the package that were not present before: - `MaccResource` / `MaccData` / `MaccCollection` (Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment) - `CreditResource` / `CreditData` / `CreditCollection` - `CreditSourceResource` / `CreditSourceData` / `CreditSourceCollection` - `FreeServicesResource` / `FreeServicesData` / `FreeServicesCollection` - `ConditionalCreditResource` / `ConditionalCreditData` - `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` / `ConditionalCreditContributorData` - `ContributorResource` - `DiscountResource` / `DiscountCollection` The pre-existing resources (`BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource`, `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource`) remain. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG empty for v1.0.0-beta.5**: The unreleased changelog entry has no content despite the significant TypeSpec migration adding 8+ new resource types - **README Key Concepts section is generic**: Only links to the Azure SDK intro page; does not describe any of the actual BillingBenefits resources (SavingsPlans, Reservations, MACC, Credits, FreeServices, ConditionalCredits, Discounts, Contributors) or what each is used for - **README Examples section lacks code snippets**: Only links to a generic samples page; no inline code examples demonstrating common operations like listing savings plans, getting MACC details, or managing credits - **No sample test files exist**: The `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subdirectory and no `Sample*.cs` files, so no test-backed snippets are available to populate the README - **New resource types (MACC, Credits, FreeServices, Discounts, etc.) are completely undocumented**: None of the 8 new resource types added by the migration are mentioned in the README 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit Update the `## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)` section to document the TypeSpec migration and new resource types: ```markdown ## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Migrated package generation from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec. - Added support for `MaccResource` to manage Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC) resources. - Added support for `CreditResource` and `CreditSourceResource` to manage billing credits and credit sources. - Added support for `FreeServicesResource` to list and manage free service entitlements. - Added support for `ConditionalCreditResource` and `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` to manage conditional credits and their contributors. - Added support for `ContributorResource` to manage billing benefit contributors. - Added support for `DiscountResource` to manage billing discounts. ``` **File 2: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md`** — Edit Replace the generic Key Concepts and Examples sections with content specific to this package: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### BillingBenefits resources `Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits` provides management operations for the following resource types: | Resource | Description | |----------|-------------| | `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource` | Represents a savings plan order that groups one or more savings plans. | | `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource` | Represents an individual savings plan within a savings plan order. | | `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a savings plan order. | | `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a reservation order. | | `MaccResource` | Represents a Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC). | | `CreditResource` | Represents a billing credit associated with a billing account. | | `CreditSourceResource` | Represents the source of a billing credit. | | `FreeServicesResource` | Represents free service entitlements for a billing account. | | `ConditionalCreditResource` | Represents a conditional credit for a billing account. | | `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` | Represents a contributor to a conditional credit. | | `ContributorResource` | Represents a billing benefits contributor. | | `DiscountResource` | Represents a discount associated with a billing account. | For more information about Azure savings plans, see [Azure savings plans overview]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/tests/Samples/Sample1_SavingsPlans.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_SavingsPlans { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task GetSavingsPlanOrders() { #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlanOrders ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); // List all savings plan orders accessible to the subscription await foreach (BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order in subscription.GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrdersAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Savings Plan Order: {order.Data.Name}, State: {order.Data.ProvisioningState}"); } #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task GetSavingsPlan() { #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlan ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); string savingsPlanOrderId = ""; string savingsPlanId = ""; // Get a specific savings plan order BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order = await subscription .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAsync(savingsPlanOrderId); // Get a specific savings plan within the order BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource savingsPlan = await order .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanAsync(savingsPlanId); Console.WriteLine($"Savings Plan: {savingsPlan.Data.Name}"); Console.WriteLine($"Term: {savingsPlan.Data.Properties?.Term}"); #endregion } } } ``` Add snippet placeholders to the README `Examples` section: ```markdown ## Examples ### List savings plan orders ``` ``` ### Get a savings plan ``` ``` For more code examples, see [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md` currently has all required sections but several need content improvements: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but install command uses `--prerelease`; keep as-is 2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — present but generic; replace with service-specific resource table (see Step 1) 3. ⚠️ **Examples** — present but has no code snippets; add inline examples (see Step 2) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Remove the standalone `## Documentation` section (lines 35–41) as this is not part of the standard README template and its links are already covered by other sections. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 billingbenefits` — injects code from `#region Snippet:` blocks into README placeholders 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 billingbenefits` — no new public types were added but run to confirm API surface is current 5. `dotnet format sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24179967966/agentic_workflow) · ● 466.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57987","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T08:31:15Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T08:31:15Z"}

This migration added **8 new resource types** to the package that were not present before: - `MaccResource` / `MaccData` / `MaccCollection` (Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment) - `CreditResource` / `CreditData` / `CreditCollection` - `CreditSourceResource` / `CreditSourceData` / `CreditSourceCollection` - `FreeServicesResource` / `FreeServicesData` / `FreeServicesCollection` - `ConditionalCreditResource` / `ConditionalCreditData` - `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` / `ConditionalCreditContributorData` - `ContributorResource` - `DiscountResource` / `DiscountCollection` The pre-existing resources (`BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource`, `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource`) remain. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG empty for v1.0.0-beta.5**: The unreleased changelog entry has no content despite the significant TypeSpec migration adding 8+ new resource types - **README Key Concepts section is generic**: Only links to the Azure SDK intro page; does not describe any of the actual BillingBenefits resources (SavingsPlans, Reservations, MACC, Credits, FreeServices, ConditionalCredits, Discounts, Contributors) or what each is used for - **README Examples section lacks code snippets**: Only links to a generic samples page; no inline code examples demonstrating common operations like listing savings plans, getting MACC details, or managing credits - **No sample test files exist**: The `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subdirectory and no `Sample*.cs` files, so no test-backed snippets are available to populate the README - **New resource types (MACC, Credits, FreeServices, Discounts, etc.) are completely undocumented**: None of the 8 new resource types added by the migration are mentioned in the README
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit Update the `## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)` section to document the TypeSpec migration and new resource types: ```markdown ## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Migrated package generation from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec. - Added support for `MaccResource` to manage Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC) resources. - Added support for `CreditResource` and `CreditSourceResource` to manage billing credits and credit sources. - Added support for `FreeServicesResource` to list and manage free service entitlements. - Added support for `ConditionalCreditResource` and `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` to manage conditional credits and their contributors. - Added support for `ContributorResource` to manage billing benefit contributors. - Added support for `DiscountResource` to manage billing discounts. ``` **File 2: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md`** — Edit Replace the generic Key Concepts and Examples sections with content specific to this package: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### BillingBenefits resources `Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits` provides management operations for the following resource types: | Resource | Description | |----------|-------------| | `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource` | Represents a savings plan order that groups one or more savings plans. | | `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource` | Represents an individual savings plan within a savings plan order. | | `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a savings plan order. | | `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a reservation order. | | `MaccResource` | Represents a Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC). | | `CreditResource` | Represents a billing credit associated with a billing account. | | `CreditSourceResource` | Represents the source of a billing credit. | | `FreeServicesResource` | Represents free service entitlements for a billing account. | | `ConditionalCreditResource` | Represents a conditional credit for a billing account. | | `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` | Represents a contributor to a conditional credit. | | `ContributorResource` | Represents a billing benefits contributor. | | `DiscountResource` | Represents a discount associated with a billing account. | For more information about Azure savings plans, see [Azure savings plans overview]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/tests/Samples/Sample1_SavingsPlans.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_SavingsPlans { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task GetSavingsPlanOrders() { #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlanOrders ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); // List all savings plan orders accessible to the subscription await foreach (BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order in subscription.GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrdersAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Savings Plan Order: {order.Data.Name}, State: {order.Data.ProvisioningState}"); } #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task GetSavingsPlan() { #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlan ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); string savingsPlanOrderId = ""; string savingsPlanId = ""; // Get a specific savings plan order BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order = await subscription .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAsync(savingsPlanOrderId); // Get a specific savings plan within the order BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource savingsPlan = await order .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanAsync(savingsPlanId); Console.WriteLine($"Savings Plan: {savingsPlan.Data.Name}"); Console.WriteLine($"Term: {savingsPlan.Data.Properties?.Term}"); #endregion } } } ``` Add snippet placeholders to the README `Examples` section: ```markdown ## Examples ### List savings plan orders ```C# Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlanOrders ``` ### Get a savings plan ```C# Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlan ``` For more code examples, see [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md` currently has all required sections but several need content improvements: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present but install command uses `--prerelease`; keep as-is 2. ⚠️ **Key concepts** — present but generic; replace with service-specific resource table (see Step 1) 3. ⚠️ **Examples** — present but has no code snippets; add inline examples (see Step 2) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ✅ **Next steps** — present 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present Remove the standalone `## Documentation` section (lines 35–41) as this is not part of the standard README template and its links are already covered by other sections. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 billingbenefits` — injects code from `#region Snippet:` blocks into README placeholders 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 billingbenefits` — no new public types were added but run to confirm API surface is current 5. `dotnet format sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/`
